title: fnp-python: Vectorize.__call__ and FromPyFunc.__call__ reject positional args (missing *args signature)
description: Calling a np.vectorize() or np.frompyfunc() result with normal positional args raises TypeError ('ndarray object is not an instance of tuple' / 'takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given'). Both __call__ methods (crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs ~7676 FromPyFunc, ~7761 Vectorize) take args: &Bound<PyTuple> without #[pyo3(signature=(*args))], so pyo3 expects a single literal tuple instead of packing positionals. Renders both top-level features unusable. Fix: add #[pyo3(signature=(*args))] to both. Found via differential probe vs numpy 2.4.3.
close_reason: Fixed in 8d06cc0a: added #[pyo3(signature=(*args))] to both Vectorize.__call__ and FromPyFunc.__call__. Calling np.vectorize()/np.frompyfunc() results with positional args now works (was always TypeError). Regression test added; verified vs numpy 2.4.3.

title: [perf] fnp-ufunc: parallelize transcendental unary ufuncs over large arrays (bit-exact)
description: elementwise_unary maps op.apply (scalar libm) serially over the array. For transcendentals (exp/log/sin/cos/tan/sinh/.../arc*/cbrt/i0) on large arrays this is compute-bound and a vs-numpy gap (numpy vectorizes). LEVER: rayon par_chunks_mut over the output for is_parallel_worth() ops when len>=32768; per-chunk FloatErrorFlags unioned (order-independent kind set -> identical dispatch). Bit-exact: same scalar op.apply per element, output filled in element order. Gate excludes cheap/memory-bound ops. Proof: perf_unary_parallel example asserts bit-identical vs inline serial + FNV; re-bench Score>=2.0.
notes: Shipped: rayon par_chunks_mut over output for is_parallel_worth() transcendental unary ops when len>=32768 (per-chunk FloatErrorFlags unioned -> identical dispatch). Bit-exact (parallel_unary_matches_serial_bitwise_on_large_arrays + parallel_unary_raises_float_errors_like_serial; perf_unary_parallel asserts bit-identical vs serial). rch +avx2 32-core, 4M elem: Exp 1.94x (423 Melem/s), Sin 3.07x (349), Log 2.48x (575). Memory-bandwidth-limited at large n but solid. 2089 lib tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Propagate NaN in non-spectral condition numbers
description:
## Problem
`cond_p_nxn` currently routes non-spectral orders (`fro`, `1`, `-1`, `inf`, `-inf`) through `inv_nxn`, which rejects non-finite inputs. NumPy instead returns `nan` for these orders when the input contains `NaN` values.

## Evidence
- `np.linalg.cond([[nan,1],[2,3]], "fro") -> nan`
- same for orders `1`, `-1`, `inf`, `-inf`
- current Rust path errors before any norm product can be formed

## Goal
Match NumPy by propagating `NaN` for non-spectral condition-number orders without changing existing SVD-based error behavior for `p=None`, `2`, and `-2`.

## Files
- `crates/fnp-linalg/src/lib.rs`
close_reason: Completed

title: Compilation failure in fnp-conformance: polyder and polyint called with arguments
description: The recent additions to crates/fnp-conformance/src/lib.rs for polynomial metamorphic testing (poly_family_deriv, poly_family_deriv_m, poly_family_int) call p.polyder(1), p.polyder(m), and p.polyint(1) on UFuncArray. However, UFuncArray::polyder and polyint take no arguments (they only compute the 1st order). This breaks the build across the workspace (cargo test --all-targets fails with E0061). Fix the test harness to either chain polyder() or update fnp-ufunc to support the m parameter. Additionally, fix the unused variable 'zero' in poly_neg_involution.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 7e82db2: polyder/polyint calls corrected to use no-argument form with loops for multi-order; unused 'zero' variable removed from poly_neg_involution. All tests pass.

title: fix(fnp): np.trace int64/uint64 precision loss — f64 accumulation returned wrong values for |diag|>2^53
description: fnp.trace summed the extracted diagonal in f64 (UFuncArray::trace + fnp-python 2-D fast path), losing precision once a diagonal element exceeds 2^53 and never carrying the exact-integer sidecar. For int64/uint64 this returned garbage (trace([[i64::MAX,_],[_,i64::MAX],...]) -> 0) where NumPy accumulates in the native int accumulator with two's-complement wraparound (-> 6). Narrower ints (int8/16/32, uint8/16/32) fit f64 losslessly and matched. Found via broad fnp-vs-numpy tobytes+dtype differential (trace 39/155 divergences). FIX: shared UFuncArray::sum_extracted_diagonal_to_scalar() folds the i64/u64 sidecar with wrapping_add and carries a fresh sidecar; f64 path unchanged. Verified 0 divergences across dtypes/offsets/non-square + int64/uint64 wraparound; unit test trace_int64_uint64_accumulates_native_with_wraparound + conformance trace_matches_numpy_accumulator_dtype green.
close_reason: Fixed in 8e9e61a2 (pending push). Shared sum_extracted_diagonal_to_scalar with wrapping i64/u64 fold; 0 divergences vs numpy; unit + conformance green.

title: [HIGH] fnp-python masked_equal rust-port: fill_value=999999 instead of compare value
description:
fnp-python test tests::masked_equal_matches_numpy_across_dtypes_and_copy FAILS on the int64-data + int-sentinel case at lib.rs:23707.

## Reproducer

export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
cargo test -p fnp-python --lib masked_equal_matches_numpy_across_dtypes_and_copy

## Observed vs expected

Input:  int64 data [1,2,3,2,5], sentinel=2
Expected (numpy.ma.masked_equal): fill_value=2
Observed (fnp_python.masked_equal): fill_value=999999 (int64 default)

## Root cause

crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:1176 — masked_scalar_compare sets fill_value via:
    let fill_value = if numpy_name == 'masked_equal' {
        scalar.values()[0]
    } else { masked_x.fill_value() };

scalar.values()[0] returns f64 via extract_precise_numeric_array. When the underlying data is int64 (as in this test), the f64 fill_value doesn't survive dtype coercion in build_numpy_masked_array — it falls back to the int64 default fill_value 999999.

## Suggested fix

In masked_scalar_compare, pass fill_value through the integer_sidecar path when the masked_x dtype is integral. Check how MaskedArray::new handles int64 fill_value: it likely needs a signed-int pass-through rather than an f64.

Alternatively, since this is the 'masked_equal' convention (numpy sets fill_value = sentinel), skip the rust-port for integer dtypes and fall back to numpy.ma.masked_equal.

## Severity rationale

[HIGH] — fill_value is user-visible in repr AND affects downstream ops like filled() which substitutes masked cells with fill_value. User code calling masked_equal(int64_arr, 2).filled(-1) would behave correctly (fill_value not used when filled has explicit arg), but masked_equal(int64_arr, 2).filled() would give 999999 instead of 2.

## Ownership

Rust-ported by cod-numpy (git blame crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:1131-1184 points to 04a14c9 'rust-port mask_or and ma_count' and cbe1cde 'rust-port masked comparator family'). Fallback path (existing wrapper) would return correct fill_value — the rust-port is what introduced the divergence.
close_reason: Closed by cc-numpy — fixed in commit (just landed). Root cause: build_numpy_masked_array discarded Rust struct fill_value; targeted fix via .set_fill_value() only on masked_equal path. Also caught and fixed allequal (true, true) match arm that was ignoring fill_value for both-masked entries. All 3 tests pass.

title: [perf][no-gaps] route np.inner through blocked GEMM + SIMD vdot reduction (tensordot siblings)
description:
Found via superlinear sweep (.probe/gemm_sibs.py) after shipping the tensordot GEMM gate (7807e066).

(1) np.inner: UFuncArray::inner (fnp-ufunc lib.rs ~13086) for ND uses a NAIVE row-parallel triple loop (sum += a[off+k]*b[off+k]), NOT the cache-blocked matmul_accumulate. inner(a,b) equals a @ b.T. Measured 11.88x at N=60 (per-call overhead, microsecond scale) and 1.61x at N=240. Options: (a) route through matmul_accumulate by transposing B to (k,n) then NN-GEMM -- C[i,j]=sum_k a[i,k]*bT[k,j]=sum_k a[i,k]*b[j,k], SAME ascending-k order so bit-identical to the naive (golden-lockable); gains blocked/microkernel speed. (b) apply the SAME metadata flop/dim gate as matmul/tensordot to delegate non-competitive sizes to numpy.inner. Prefer (a)+(b) combined.

(2) np.vdot: SUPERLINEAR 0.78x at 200K to 8.15x at 1.6M. UFuncArray::vdot (fnp-ufunc ~13385) is iter().zip().map(a*b).sum() -- a sequential dependent-chain f64 add, no SIMD/ILP. numpy vdot is BLAS ddot (SIMD+FMA). Fix: chunked multi-accumulator pairwise sum for ILP/SIMD. PARITY CAVEAT: changes f64 sum order vs current sequential; numpy vdot is BLAS (non-reproducible) so allclose is the only contract -- but check no existing golden/bit test locks vdot order first. Same concern for vecdot.

Both lower-value than tensordot (inner mild + overhead-dominated; vdot bites only at >1M). matmul/dot/tensordot/einsum already GEMM-routed. dot_stack (batched 3D matmul) measured ~1x.
notes: np.inner GEMM-gate SHIPPED (840b2595): mirrors the tensordot fix — metadata pre-gate delegates non-competitive inner contractions to numpy, keeps native only in the sweet spot. n=128 8.30x->1.20x, n=256 6.24x->0.95x (delegate); n=400 0.14x / n=512 0.45x (native beats numpy). NOTE: routing inner through matmul_accumulate (B-transpose) was REJECTED first — measured 0.98x vs the naive contiguous-dot row-parallel loop. CORRECTION 2026-06-05 BlackThrush: pushed vdot sequential-unroll commit 807df6ec was remeasured and rejected. RCH ts1 exact-state vdot_unrolled_loop_ab_bench produced VDOT_CORE len=1600000 new=1.203ms old=1.200ms speedup=1.00x, conflicting with the earlier vmi1153651 1.29x and below the Score>=2.0 keep gate. Behavior proof SHA for the rejected candidate was 9d2754708892adfb99ddb0002f66c2be2a1806366997dee279ed3e281a562c81; rollback commit restores the prior iterator reduction so no failed vdot code is kept. Next primitive is franken_numpy-3q3pe (+fma/tolerance-parity audit) targeting FMA-class GEMM/dot wins rather than more iterator micro-tuning.
close_reason: Completed: np.inner GEMM metadata gate already shipped; vdot core reduction now unrolls the exact same sequential accumulation order. rch same-worker vmi1153651 A/B VDOT_CORE len=1600000 old=4.203ms new=3.260ms speedup=1.29x; golden SHA256 9d2754708892adfb99ddb0002f66c2be2a1806366997dee279ed3e281a562c81; public conformance_vdot passed 7/7 with existing DISC-011 ignore.

title: [perf][no-gaps] Row-block safe-Rust matmul while preserving FP-bit order
description:
Profile target: fnp-ufunc UFuncArray::matmul dense F64 GEMM path. Existing cross-engine baseline reports matmul_medium FNP p50 39.434 us vs NumPy/OpenBLAS p50 11.602 us, ratio 3.3989x slower. Fresh rch-invoked Criterion provisional baseline for core_ops/matmul_256x256_by_256x256: 3.0226 ms center, CI [2.9527, 3.0802]; rch fell back locally because no admissible workers, so before/after must be confirmed via rch rerun before commit acceptance.

Alien primitive: communication-avoiding/cache-blocked dense linear algebra, narrowed to a safe-Rust row-blocked GEMM microkernel. First lever must preserve each output cell accumulation order exactly: k increases 0..K-1, with identical a*b additions per output. No unsafe, no BLAS linkage.

Acceptance: one lever only; golden-output sha256 and bitwise matmul proof pass; focused fnp-ufunc tests/check/clippy/fmt via rch where applicable; re-benchmark via rch confirms Score>=2.0 before commit.
notes: Accepted ROW_BLOCK=8 safe-Rust row-blocked matmul lever. Profile-backed target core_ops/matmul_256x256_by_256x256. Baseline rch remote vmi1293453 before edit: 3.2649/3.3529/3.4565 ms. Row-block=4 was rejected as marginal/noisy on same worker. Kept after rch remote pinned vmi1293453: 1.7293/1.7652/1.8259 ms (47.4% median reduction). Isomorphism: per-output k-order unchanged; no RNG/tie-breaking/error shape changes; golden SHA-256 dd19822c9ba24f9a17d5c6f3e112451265c4ac4659cd5d8e78de97c77f7c2df7 passed in matmul_row_blocking_preserves_scalar_accumulation_bits. Alien primitive: communication-avoiding/cache-local BLAS-3 inner kernel row-block reuse; safe Rust only, no unsafe/no external BLAS. Score Impact 5 x Confidence 5 / Effort 2 = 12.5. Validation so far: rch cargo test -p fnp-ufunc matmul --lib passed 19 tests; rch cargo check -p fnp-ufunc --all-targets passed; rch cargo clippy -p fnp-ufunc --all-targets -- -D warnings passed.
close_reason: Completed safe-Rust ROW_BLOCK=8 matmul row-block lever. Baseline rch vmi1293453: 3.2649/3.3529/3.4565 ms; after rch pinned vmi1293453: 1.7293/1.7652/1.8259 ms (47.4% median reduction). Isomorphism: per-output k-order unchanged and golden SHA-256 dd19822c9ba24f9a17d5c6f3e112451265c4ac4659cd5d8e78de97c77f7c2df7. Validation: rch matmul tests 19 passed; rch cargo check -p fnp-ufunc --all-targets passed; rch cargo clippy -p fnp-ufunc --all-targets -- -D warnings passed.

title: [perf][fnp-python] zero-copy f64 np.modf tuple boundary
description: Current in-flight diff adds a zero-copy exact float64 ndarray fast path for np.modf, analogous to the shipped frexp tuple boundary fast path. Target: eliminate extract_numeric_array -> ufunc_modf -> two array build for C-contiguous f64 ndarrays by reading PyBuffer<ReadOnlyCell<f64>> and writing fractional/integral float64 output arrays directly. Baseline/proof plan: establish clean-HEAD rch Criterion baseline from a detached worktree; add a focused Criterion lane for fnp/numpy modf f64 1M; prove behavior unchanged against NumPy for signed zero, negative values, integer values, infinities, NaNs, subnormals, empty and shaped arrays with golden sha256; run crate-scoped fnp-python tests/check/clippy/fmt; rebench same worker; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Kept zero-copy f64 np.modf tuple-boundary fast path. Baseline via rch clean-HEAD scratch/local fallback: fnp_modf_f64_1m 22.950/23.240/23.514 ms, NumPy 2.0062/2.0323/2.0676 ms. After via rch same local fallback host: fnp_modf_f64_1m 511.62/544.08/579.51 us, NumPy 1.9190/1.9370/1.9713 ms; 42.7x center speedup over FNP baseline and 3.56x faster than NumPy. Isomorphism: scalar/unsupported layouts defer; output order and tuple order unchanged; no RNG/tie surface; FP proof locked by golden SHA256 ee45bb6c4a9a21df366f3698b20d11a1a1fb41a91986f8b296c2fb7b56b3ec7b over fractional/integral dtype+shape+raw bytes for signed zero, negatives, integers, inf, nan, subnormal, empty and shaped arrays. Validation: rch cargo test -p fnp-python --test conformance_frexp_modf modf_ passed; rch cargo test -p fnp-python modf_ passed; rch cargo check -p fnp-python --all-targets passed; rch cargo clippy -p fnp-python --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; git diff --check passed. Score=Impact 5 x Confidence 0.95 / Effort 1 = 4.75.

title: [perf][no-gaps][fnp-linalg] divide-and-conquer bidiagonal SVD core
description: Pass-2 profile after modf closeout: crate-scoped RCH local fallback linalg profile ranks svd_mxn_full/size/512 highest at 175.60/178.64/182.20 ms, ahead of qr_nxn/512 113.63/116.59/120.54 ms, eigvalsh_nxn/512 98.333/99.954/101.55 ms, svd_nxn/512 88.895/90.142/91.525 ms, batch_eigvalsh/16x256x256 74.295/77.708/81.269 ms, and matrix_power_nxn/1024 68.080/76.002/82.934 ms. Recent reflector replay/Givens replay/compact-WY replay attempts closed no-ship, so this bead deliberately switches primitive family. Alien-graveyard basis: Communication-Avoiding Algorithms section 9.6 (Demmel/Ballard CA-QR/TSQR) plus alien-artifact numerical-core route: divide-and-conquer bidiagonal SVD / secular merge for large full SVD, with fallback to existing Golub-Reinsch/Jacobi path on proof failure. One lever only: introduce the large-square bidiagonal D&C core or a tightly scoped prerequisite for that core; do not touch Python wrappers, generic GEMM, QR iteration replay, or eigvalsh. Proof obligations: finite-input rejection unchanged; singular values descending; U/Vt orthogonality and reconstruction within existing tolerances; high-condition small singular value test remains protected (no A^T*A route); exact SHA tests either remain stable for untouched small cases or are updated only with documented tolerance-contract approval; same-worker RCH baseline/rebench; keep only Score>=2.0.
close_reason: Measured no-ship. Candidate primitive was guarded tridiag-Gram full SVD route: form B^T B from the bidiagonal, solve the symmetric tridiagonal eigenproblem, sort singular values, rotate Vt, and reconstruct U. Proof before rejection: focused SHA/reconstruction test passed after digest e1d4c1622015155454b5f0341a12f25e32d03255c388783b2794dc7dcad5efe8; rch cargo test -p fnp-linalg svd passed 25 unit + 2 conformance + 2 golden + 2 metamorphic SVD tests. Rebench failed against clean current-HEAD scratch baseline: clean HEAD local fallback svd_mxn_full/512 106.05/107.49/109.11 ms; candidate after local fallback 106.13/108.58/110.97 ms and Criterion reported no win/regression. Score < 2.0; source/test hunk reverted. Next route should avoid A^T A / Gram-family SVD and target a true bidiagonal QR/QL core replacement or secular divide-and-conquer implementation with lower overhead.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg] batch eigvalsh 256 blocked tridiag crossover
description: Pass-2 RCH linalg profile on vmi1149989 after modf closeout ranks batch_eigvalsh/16x256x256 as the top row: 142.39/152.54/163.75 ms, ahead of svd_mxn_full/512 101.67/110.38/119.07 ms, eigvalsh_nxn/512 93.819/97.058/99.727 ms, matrix_power_nxn/1024 79.461/85.854/92.212 ms, and svd_nxn/512 74.804/78.765/82.525 ms. batch_eigvalsh already parallelizes lanes through batch_map_lanes, so the next lever targets the per-lane eigvalsh core. One lever: lower the values-only blocked symmetric tridiagonalization crossover so the profiled n=256 lanes use the existing dsytrd-style compact panel/rank-2k reducer rather than the unblocked per-column sweep. Alien primitive: vectorized/morsel batch profile plus communication-avoiding blocked symmetric tridiagonalization. Proof obligations: sorted eigenvalue order unchanged, finite/error surface unchanged, no RNG/tie surface, batch lane order and lowest-index error behavior unchanged, golden/tolerance evidence against existing eigvalsh/batch tests. Rebench via RCH same crate before keeping; keep only Score>=2.0, otherwise revert source hunk and close no-ship.
close_reason: Measured no-ship. Profile target was batch_eigvalsh/16x256x256, top row on RCH vmi1149989 at 142.39/152.54/163.75 ms; same-session comparable vmi1156319 baseline before lever was 76.464/80.155/83.819 ms. One lever tested: lower TRIDIAG_BLOCK_MIN 384 -> 256 so profiled n=256 lanes route through existing blocked symmetric tridiagonalization. Proof before rejection: rch cargo test -p fnp-linalg blocked_tridiag_matches_unblocked passed; rch cargo test -p fnp-linalg batch_lanes_parallel_match_serial_reference_and_golden_sha256 passed, preserving lane order and digest 899c3036487b23b734395f17af0b62dc783bc1c4d9969993308eaf2a58af86b1 for n=128 batch golden. Rebench on vmi1156319 after lever: 100.43/109.46/118.63 ms, slower than same-worker baseline. Score < 2.0; source hunk reverted. Next route: do not repeat crossover tweaks; attack eigvalsh core with a fundamentally different primitive such as specialized symmetric rank-2k packing or tridiagonal QR/QL core replacement.

title: [perf][fnp-python] all-width zero-copy np.putmask
description: Pass-3 target after linalg rejection streak and no ready perf child: Python boundary np.putmask still has only an f64 zero-copy fast path; fixed-width narrow ints, int64/uint64, float32, and bool fall through extract_numeric_array/copy-back and can widen through f64. Existing dirty candidate adds same-width unsigned-view in-place scatter for identical dtype arrays. Baseline/proof plan: add focused Criterion lane for uint8/int32/float32 putmask vs NumPy; baseline clean HEAD via rch; prove behavior unchanged with dtype/shape/raw-byte golden SHA for bool/int/float signed-zero/NaN and fallback parity for mismatched dtype/shape/complex; rebench via rch and keep only Score>=2.0. One lever only: same-width unsigned-view putmask scatter; no fmin/fmax signed-zero work.
close_reason: Kept via commit 755877ac plus focused proof/bench evidence. One lever: same-width unsigned-view in-place np.putmask scatter for same-dtype bool/int/float arrays; f64 dedicated helper and fallback paths remain first/unchanged. Same-worker RCH vmi1156319 Criterion baseline from clean-HEAD scratch with benchmark-only hunk: u8 31.092/32.085/33.430 ms, i32 47.623/48.605/49.595 ms, f32 11.179/11.530/12.055 ms; NumPy u8 1.5161/1.5967/1.6992 ms. Same-worker candidate: u8 1.2850/1.3291/1.3681 ms, i32 1.8666/1.9259/1.9981 ms, f32 1.9332/1.9797/2.0188 ms; NumPy u8 1.4849/1.5423/1.6295 ms. Speedups: u8 24.1x, i32 25.2x, f32 5.8x; u8 now 1.16x faster than NumPy on same worker. Behavior proof: ordering is flat-index order; value cycling preserves numpy a.flat[i]=values.flat[i%len(values)]; tie-breaking/RNG N/A; FP/raw bytes preserved by same-width unsigned view, including signed zero, NaN, inf; golden SHA c0e0c54ade3ec56cb1c4eb82544e4d8726ddb86a7836dbd325b502294af5bd9c. RCH proof: cargo test -p fnp-python --test conformance_extract_put putmask_zerocopy passed remotely on vmi1156319; cargo test -p fnp-python --test conformance_extract_put putmask passed via RCH local fallback 7/7; cargo check -p fnp-python --all-targets passed remotely on vmi1149989; cargo clippy -p fnp-python --all-targets -- -D warnings passed remotely on vmi1149989; git diff --check clean. Format/UBS notes: cargo fmt -p fnp-python -- --check remains blocked by broad existing src/lib.rs formatting drift; UBS targeted scan remains nonzero from inherited benchmark/test inventories, not a putmask-specific blocker.

Do not alter SVD, eigvalsh, generic GEMM, QR arithmetic inside either algorithm, Python wrappers, or tests except proof goldens if needed. close_reason: Measured no-ship. Target qr_nxn/size/512 from local-fallback reprofile was 73.422/74.481/75.596 ms; focused RCH baseline before edit on vmi1156319 was 151.37/158.72/167.87 ms. title: [perf][fnp-linalg] matrix_power 1024 packed GEMM primitive
description: Pass-4 profile-backed target after clean RCH fnp-linalg Criterion reprofile on vmi1149989 at HEAD 93cdccf0: matrix_power_nxn/size/1024 = 193.34/202.34/213.51 ms, top row ahead of batch_eigvalsh/16x256x256 = 152.97/166.61/181.52 ms and svd_mxn_full/512 = 159.60/165.89/172.72 ms. Alien primitive: cache-aware/register-blocked BLAS-3 kernel improvement in pure safe Rust; no C BLAS/LAPACK/MKL/XLA, no unsafe. One lever only: improve packed GEMM/matrix_power hot path while preserving each output element's ascending-k accumulation order, row-major output ordering, NaN/signed-zero behavior, and RNG-free determinism. Proof obligations: focused golden SHA for matrix_power A^3 remains stable or is explicitly justified by unchanged exact operation order; existing mat_mul_flat serial-reference bit test passes; RCH cargo test -p fnp-linalg matrix_power/mat_mul proof filters pass; RCH focused Criterion baseline/rebench on same worker required; keep only Score>=2.0, otherwise revert source hunk and close no-ship.
close_reason: Measured no-ship. Target came from clean RCH linalg profile on vmi1149989: matrix_power_nxn/size/1024 = 193.34/202.34/213.51 ms, top row. Focused clean-before RCH baseline on vmi1156319 scratch = 217.82/240.38/265.54 ms; local fallback before via rch = 28.416/28.671/29.019 ms. Next route: do not repeat B-pack hoisting; attack a deeper GEMM primitive such as different row-band granularity/cache-oblivious recursive tiling or an exact-safe microkernel ownership/buffer-reuse model with same ascending-k proof.

title: [perf][fnp-python] Close np.average(axis=1) unweighted f64 residual with proof-safe row kernel
description: Profile-backed target from current RCH crate-scoped Criterion run on vmi1153651 at HEAD 93cdccf0. Row: python_average_nansum_axis_boundary/fnp_average_axis1_unweighted_f64_2048x512 = 1.2097/1.2814/1.4214 ms vs numpy_average_axis1_unweighted_f64_2048x512 = 688.66/786.36/851.83 us, about 1.63x slower center. Related rows: weighted average is at parity/faster (2.8044 ms vs NumPy 3.1766 ms), nansum is faster than NumPy, histogram i64 faster, histogram f32 parity, float_power gap is larger absolute but lower-confidence pow/libm semantics. Primitive: graveyard §8.2 vectorized execution / §9.6 communication-avoiding row kernels applied as a proof-safe row-reduction lever; one change only, preserving current scalar summation order or proving golden tolerance if not bit-exact. Proof obligations: axis/order unchanged, dtype float64 unchanged, returned/weights/keepdims fallback unchanged, empty/error handling fallback unchanged, FP order documented, golden sha256 over dtype/shape/raw bytes for representative finite/zero/NaN/inf rows. title: [perf][fnp-linalg] portable-SIMD packed GEMM microkernel for matrix_power
description: Profile-backed target after .105 packed-B hoist and .107 Python min/max SIMD no-ships. Existing packed_gemm uses a scalar 4x8 register tile over a packed B micropanel; every output element accumulates k ascending. Alien/no-gaps primitive: safe portable-SIMD BLAS-3 microkernel, not C BLAS/LAPACK/MKL/XLA and not another B-pack ownership tweak. One lever only: replace the scalar PACKED_MR x PACKED_NR inner accumulator with a safe std::simd 4x8 lane accumulator (or reject if feature/proof/bench fails). Proof obligations: per-lane ascending-k FP order preserved; row-major output order unchanged; NaN/signed-zero behavior unchanged because each lane performs the same multiply then add sequence for every k; RNG/tie N/A; existing mat_mul_flat/mat_mul_rect SHA tests and matrix_power SHA/fallback tests pass. Gate: focused RCH baseline before edit, rebench same worker or same-host clean scratch; keep only Score>=2.0, otherwise revert and close no-ship.
close_reason: Measured no-ship. title: perf(fnp-ufunc): fused-workspace Winograd/Strassen large-square GEMM
description:
Follow-up to franken_numpy-qr6h2 after the allocation-heavy one-level standard Strassen screen was rejected/no-shipped. Profile-backed context: current matmul_accumulate is the large-square GEMM kernel for matmul/dot/tensordot/cov/einsum; small bit-goldens lock exact classical accumulation. Remote/provisional evidence from qr6h2: ovh-a perf_gemm_pack_probe showed tile tweaks are too small (4x16 vs 4x8 at n2304 only 1.13x), and the one-level standard Strassen screen changed large-output bits and failed to produce stable same-worker Score>=2.0; local candidate n2048 was about 121ms vs classical about 51ms. Do not repeat naive Strassen with seven product buffers or MR/KC tile tweaks.

Target primitive: fused workspace Winograd/Strassen for large square finite f64 GEMM, using direct quadrant views where possible, one reusable product buffer, preallocated scratch arena, fewer additions than standard Strassen, and threshold n>=4096 unless a same-worker crossover proves n3072/2048. Keep pure safe Rust; no C BLAS/LAPACK/MKL.

Proof obligations: existing small exact-bit SHA tests must remain unchanged by thresholding; finite-only guard for lhs/rhs/out; shape/error ordering unchanged; no RNG/tie semantics; tolerance envelope vs current classical output and live NumPy for representative plus adversarial finite matrices; deterministic large-output digest for the accelerated path; scratch cap and fallback to classical on cap/nonfinite/unsupported shape. Benchmark gate: same-worker rch before/after, Score>=2.0 only, target >=1.7x at n4096 or reject and route to cache-oblivious exact classical GEMM or another sub-cubic primitive.
close_reason: Measured no-ship for one-level fused-workspace standard Strassen screen. Baseline RCH local fallback RAYON_NUM_THREADS=16 n4096 production matmul was 479.074 ms checksum 0x6f9ecdcfa0dc7958. Candidate kept small exact-bit lock but changed large checksum to 0x962c3353f6467a1b and regressed to 831.052 ms, Score <2.0. Source hunk removed/stashed; no production GEMM change retained. Next route is a different primitive: exact-order portable-SIMD/classical GEMM microkernel or cache-oblivious exact classical layout, not one-level standard Strassen.

Proof before benchmark: RCH local fallback cargo check -p fnp-ufunc --lib passed, and cargo test -p fnp-ufunc --release matmul_row_blocking_preserves_scalar_accumulation_bits -- --nocapture passed, preserving the small exact-bit lock because the gate is n4096. Same-mode benchmark: baseline from pass 1 was RCH local fallback RAYON_NUM_THREADS=16 n4096 479.074 ms checksum 0x6f9ecdcfa0dc7958; candidate rebench was RCH local fallback RAYON_NUM_THREADS=16 n4096 831.052 ms checksum 0x962c3353f6467a1b. This is a 1.73x regression, Score <2.0, so source hunk is being removed. Next route should not repeat one-level standard Strassen; attack a different primitive such as exact cache-oblivious classical GEMM, portable-SIMD/explicit non-FMA register kernels, or a deeper Winograd schedule that materially reduces memory traffic before multiplication.

title: [perf][fnp-ufunc] exact-order portable-SIMD GEMM microkernel for large square matmul
description: Follow-up after franken_numpy-ixs5y.117 no-ship. Profile-backed target remains production fnp-ufunc large-square matmul_accumulate: RCH local-fallback baseline with RAYON_NUM_THREADS=16 n4096 was 479.074 ms / 286.88 GFLOP/s, checksum 0x6f9ecdcfa0dc7958. One-level fused-workspace standard Strassen was rejected: same-mode candidate 831.052 ms checksum 0x962c3353f6467a1b despite preserving small exact-bit lock, so do not repeat one-level Strassen or seven-product workspace variants.\n\nTarget primitive: exact-order safe-Rust classical GEMM improvement, preferably portable SIMD/register microkernel or cache-oblivious row/column tiling that preserves each output element's strictly increasing k accumulation order and avoids FMA/tolerance-contract changes. No C BLAS/LAPACK/MKL. Baseline first via rch crate-scoped n4096 harness and any focused criterion/hyperfine row; implement one lever only; prove matmul_row_blocking SHA and einsum GEMM bit locks unchanged plus n4096 checksum stability if exact-order; rebench same worker/mode; keep only Score>=2.0, otherwise close measured no-ship and route to the next algorithmic primitive.
close_reason: Completed: shipped exact-order portable-SIMD GEMM tile. Same-worker RCH remote ovh-a baseline n4096 3239.541 ms / 42.43 GFLOP/s checksum 0x6f9ecdcfa0dc7958; candidate 1202.691 ms / 114.28 GFLOP/s checksum unchanged (2.69x). Local fallback sanity also improved 479.074 ms -> 415.066 ms. Isomorphism: output shape/order/error/RNG unaffected, packed-B/blocking structure unchanged, each output lane still accumulates kk strictly increasing; large checksum unchanged; strengthened matmul SHA test crosses SIMD tile with digest 204cc8437804c41b22ab6bc1de6ab3820e4f2eed62a83fa17cbb78de5dbff2db; einsum GEMM bit proof passed. Validation: cargo check -p fnp-ufunc --lib passed; cargo fmt -p fnp-ufunc --check passed; focused matmul/einsum release tests passed. Clippy all-targets still has unrelated pre-existing fnp-ufunc lints and was not widened. Score 2.56.

comments.author: BlackThrush
comments.text: 2026-06-08 BlackThrush keep: exact-order portable-SIMD GEMM tile. Baseline from clean scratch f0e73dee via RCH remote ovh-a, RAYON_NUM_THREADS=16, cargo run -p fnp-ufunc --release --example perf_strassen_baseline -- 4096: n4096 3239.541 ms, 42.43 GFLOP/s, checksum 0x6f9ecdcfa0dc7958. One lever: replace the MATMUL_MR x MATMUL_NR scalar accumulator tile with std::simd::Simd<f64, 8> column lanes while preserving the outer j/i blocking, packed-B layout, and strictly increasing kk accumulation for each output lane. Candidate rebench via same worker/mode ovh-a: n4096 1202.691 ms, 114.28 GFLOP/s, checksum unchanged 0x6f9ecdcfa0dc7958 (2.69x speedup). Local fallback sanity also improved 479.074 ms -> 415.066 ms with the same checksum. Proof: rch exec -- cargo check -p fnp-ufunc --lib passed; rch exec -- cargo test -p fnp-ufunc --release matmul_row_blocking_preserves_scalar_accumulation_bits -- --nocapture passed after strengthening the test to n=16 so it crosses the SIMD tile, pinned SHA 204cc8437804c41b22ab6bc1de6ab3820e4f2eed62a83fa17cbb78de5dbff2db; rch exec -- cargo test -p fnp-ufunc --release einsum_batched_gemm_matches_manual_reference_bits -- --nocapture passed; cargo fmt -p fnp-ufunc --check passed. Clippy all-targets still fails on pre-existing fnp-ufunc lints unrelated to this hunk (dead_code interpolate_percentile_method, needless_range_loop/manual_is_multiple_of/manual_range_contains/test vec/if-same-then-else); not widened in this performance commit. Score: Impact 2.69 x Confidence 0.95 / Effort 1.0 = 2.56 keep.

title: [perf][fnp-ufunc] broadcast_to direct replication for structured broadcasts
description: Profile-backed follow-up after franken_numpy-kzs3e parallel odometer broadcast_to keep. Fresh RCH crate-scoped local-fallback baseline, RAYON_NUM_THREADS=16, cargo bench -p fnp-ufunc --bench broadcast -- --noplot: production odometer_par [1,4096]->[4096,4096] 41.313/42.197/43.227 ms; [4096,1]->[4096,4096] 44.546/45.414/46.352 ms; [256,1,256]->[256,256,256] 42.113/42.563/43.061 ms. Current implementation builds out_count source_indices then gathers values and sidecar. Target one lever: direct block replication for structured broadcasts where only whole contiguous blocks repeat (leading/interior row-block repeat) and/or contiguous scalar fills, removing source_indices allocation and per-element source-index gather for those cases. Proof: pure copy/fill must preserve output shape/order/dtype, NaN/inf/signed-zero raw bits, i64/u64 sidecar replication, no FP arithmetic/RNG/tie changes; broadcast_to_parallel_odometer_matches_naive_and_golden must keep or intentionally update only if adding coverage with exact same semantics. Benchmark gate: same command before/after, keep only Score>=2.0; otherwise revert source hunk and close measured no-ship.
close_reason: Measured no-ship. The earlier dirty pad axis-map candidate preserved semantics but failed the gate (12.653 ms -> 19.362 ms, repair 13.225 ms -> 15.207 ms), and the later uncommitted repeat(axis=...) copy-slice hunk was not the profile-backed broadcast_to target and was unwound before commit. No production code retained for franken_numpy-ixs5y.119; next active target remains franken_numpy-ixs5y.120 inner_lastaxis GEMM.

title: [perf][fnp-ufunc] route inner last-axis through exact-order GEMM
description: Profile-backed target from 2026-06-09 RCH remote ovh-a contraction criterion run at HEAD df2801e1. inner_lastaxis/256 measured [2.8378 ms, 4.1233 ms, 5.8918 ms] while tensordot_axes1/256 using matmul_accumulate measured [426.27 us, 440.64 us, 448.04 us] in the same run. Structural gap: inner(A,B) computes A @ B^T but currently runs scalar dot loops over B rows instead of reusing the packed-B portable-SIMD exact-order GEMM kernel. One lever only: materialize RHS as row-major transpose by bit-copy, route dense f64 ND inner through matmul_accumulate, and preserve each output element's strictly ascending k accumulation order. Proof gate: strengthen/run inner bit-reference golden SHA over square+rectangular finite/signed-zero/NaN values, run focused inner/matmul/einsum tests, baseline then same-worker rebench via crate-scoped rch, keep only Score>=2.0; otherwise unwind and close no-ship.

One lever tested: materialize RHS as row-major K x B transpose by bit-copy and route finite large ND inner through exact-order matmul_accumulate, preserving output shape/order/dtype/RNG-free behavior and ascending-k accumulation per cell. Proof before rejection: inner_parallel_matches_serial_reference_bits passed with candidate SHA ddc447b77c07c90949d2cebdc9a314ce92391fc524d5732a0d4db5cddd12bbe7; matmul_row_blocking_preserves_scalar_accumulation_bits passed; einsum_batched_gemm_matches_manual_reference_bits passed; cargo check -p fnp-ufunc --lib passed with only pre-existing interpolate_percentile_method dead-code warning. Same-mode rebench: [1.1801 ms, 1.1909 ms, 1.2066 ms], a 0.79x regression vs baseline. Score <2.0; source hunk unwound. Next route: direct inner B^T microkernel / panel pack without full RHS transpose, not another copy-first route.

title: [perf][fnp-ufunc] direct B^T panel microkernel for inner last-axis
description: Follow-up to franken_numpy-ixs5y.120 no-ship. Profile-backed target remains inner_lastaxis/256: original focused RCH local-fallback baseline [924.75 us, 937.19 us, 950.93 us]; full RHS K x B transpose plus matmul_accumulate regressed to [1.1801 ms, 1.1909 ms, 1.2066 ms], so do not repeat copy-first whole-RHS transposition. New primitive: direct inner-specific B^T panel microkernel / packed micro-panel route that reads RHS rows as b[j,k] and packs only the current NR-wide B-row panel into kk-major scratch inside the GEMM tile, avoiding full RHS transpose allocation/copy while preserving each output cell's strictly ascending-k accumulation order. One lever only; baseline first via the same crate-scoped RCH Criterion row; prove output shape/order/dtype/error/RNG-free behavior unchanged with inner bit-reference/golden SHA and adjacent matmul/einsum proofs; rebench same mode and keep only Score>=2.0, otherwise unwind and close no-ship.

One lever: for large finite ND inner, avoid whole-RHS transpose and pack only each NR-wide RHS-row panel into kk-major scratch inside an inner-specific SIMD tile; output shape/order/dtype/error/RNG behavior unchanged and each output cell still accumulates k strictly ascending. Rebench same command/mode: [647.25 us, 658.26 us, 668.58 us], 3.21x center speedup. Proof: inner_parallel_matches_serial_reference_bits passed with strengthened SHA 2f4b0ffc9655eff9fe6abad9c8d92dcdedbdf446c906831d7d5f8de471404458 covering finite accelerated, signed-zero finite, and NaN/inf fallback cases; matmul_row_blocking_preserves_scalar_accumulation_bits passed; einsum_batched_gemm_matches_manual_reference_bits passed; cargo check -p fnp-ufunc --lib passed on ovh-a with only pre-existing interpolate_percentile_method dead-code warning. cargo fmt -p fnp-ufunc --check remains blocked by pre-existing broadcast/pad formatting drift outside this lever. Score: Impact 3.21 x Confidence 0.95 / Effort 1.0 = 3.05 keep.

title: [perf][no-gaps][fnp-linalg] true bidiagonal D&C / two-stage full-SVD primitive

Recent no-ships rejected row-parallel/right-Householder micro-levers and the normal-equations/Gram full-SVD route ([116.18,118.95,121.96] ms baseline -> [141.85,143.20,144.68] ms candidate). description: Profile-backed follow-up to deadlock-audit-mtjkm. Baseline clean ab8fe715 on RCH vmi1152480 for python_statistics_boundary/fnp_cov_rowvar_f64_200x500 was 4.7898/5.6907/6.6160 ms; MR=4 row-block candidate was 4.1415/4.9610/5.7133 ms, only ~1.15x with overlapping intervals, so it was unwound and rejected. Diagnosis from mtjkm: row-major centered data (n_vars x n_obs) makes each lower-triangle dot reread rows repeatedly; single-dot micro-unrolling is the wrong primitive. Next lever must change data movement: build/pack centered observations into contiguous observation-major panels or equivalent transposed scratch and compute symmetric rank-k variable tiles (safe-Rust BLAS-3/SYRK-like panel kernel) so each loaded observation panel is reused across multiple Gram cells. Preserve cov/corrcoef behavior: small exact-repr tests must keep the serial path or prove allclose parity; ordering/tie-breaking/RNG unchanged; floating-point contract documented; golden sha256 required. Baseline first with RCH Criterion on cov_rowvar_f64_50x1000, 200x500, 500x500, and 50x10000; keep only Score>=2.0 and no regression in already-winning regimes. Alien-graveyard routing: communication-avoiding/tiled linear algebra and cache/layout data-movement primitives.
close_reason: Rejected/no-ship. Same-worker RCH vmi1149989, old runtime with expanded benchmark harness vs candidate packed observation-panel tile. Behavior proof passed: cargo test -p fnp-python --test conformance_statistics native_fast_path_matches_numpy --release -- --nocapture, 2 passed, cov SHA unchanged. Performance regressed: cov 50x1000 448.51 us -> 1.2138 ms; corrcoef 50x1000 816.28 us -> 840.13 us; cov 200x500 3.1097 ms -> 3.9761 ms; corrcoef 200x500 1.6408 ms -> 4.7379 ms; cov 500x500 6.4541 ms -> 21.017 ms; corrcoef 500x500 6.7173 ms -> 20.161 ms; cov 50x10000 2.2885 ms -> 9.6321 ms; corrcoef 50x10000 2.3002 ms -> 6.3470 ms. Next route: different primitive, likely lower-level safe SIMD/blocked covariance core or native_cov/matmul path selection, not observation-major tile packing.

title: [perf][fnp-python][no-gaps] cov/corrcoef packed observation panels for symmetric Gram
description:
Follow-up to deadlock-audit-mtjkm. The dot8 and MR=4 row-block attempts showed cov_gram_rowvar_f64 is not fixed by another per-row/per-dot micro-tweak: same-worker RCH vmi1152480 MR=4 was only 4.7898/5.6907/6.6160 ms baseline vs 4.1415/4.9610/5.7133 ms candidate on python_statistics_boundary/fnp_cov_rowvar_f64_200x500, with overlapping intervals and Score < 2.0.

Profile-backed root: centered is stored row-major as n_vars x n_obs, and lower-triangle Gram generation repeatedly streams observation vectors with poor panel reuse. The next primitive must change data movement: pack or transpose centered observations into contiguous panels, then compute lower-triangular symmetric-rank-k tiles with explicit panel reuse while staying pure safe Rust.

One lever only: introduce a packed/transposed observation-panel path for the large f64 rowvar cov/corrcoef fast path, gated by shape/work thresholds. Preserve small-input exact repr paths when needed. Do not touch unrelated statistics paths.

Proof obligations: (1) same-worker RCH Criterion baseline before edit and after edit for fnp_cov_rowvar_f64_200x500 plus no-regression checks on the already-good 500x500 and 50x10000 regimes if benchmark rows exist or can be added narrowly; (2) cov/corrcoef conformance tests green; (3) isomorphism proof documents ordering/tie-breaking/RNG N/A, dtype/error paths unchanged, floating-point tolerance contract or bit-stable path, and a golden sha256 where an existing golden harness applies; (4) keep only if Score >= 2.0.

Alien primitive: communication-avoiding symmetric rank-k / panel-packing data movement from the no-gaps directive and alien numerical linear algebra guidance. Expected target: at least 2x on 200x500 and material improvement on the known 50x1000 gap without regressing large regimes.
close_reason: Duplicate/superseded by franken_numpy-ixs5y.192 measured rejection. The packed/transposed observation-panel symmetric Gram candidate was tested on same-worker RCH vmi1149989 and regressed every target row: cov 50x1000 448.51 us -> 1.2138 ms; cov 200x500 3.1097 ms -> 3.9761 ms; cov 500x500 6.4541 ms -> 21.017 ms; cov 50x10000 2.2885 ms -> 9.6321 ms, with corrcoef regressions too. Runtime hunk unwound. Do not continue this route; next primitive is routing eligible f64 rowvar Python cov/corrcoef through the existing lower-level UFuncArray safe-SIMD/GEMM covariance core, with the same baseline rows.

title: [perf][fnp-python][no-gaps] route f64 rowvar cov/corrcoef through UFuncArray SIMD/GEMM core
description:
Profile-backed successor to franken_numpy-ixs5y.192/.193. The packed observation-panel tile was measured and rejected on same-worker RCH vmi1149989: cov 50x1000 448.51 us -> 1.2138 ms; cov 200x500 3.1097 ms -> 3.9761 ms; cov 500x500 6.4541 ms -> 21.017 ms; cov 50x10000 2.2885 ms -> 9.6321 ms. New primitive: stop bypassing the lower-level covariance engine. fnp-ufunc::UFuncArray::cov already has a pure safe-Rust portable-SIMD path for nvars < 128 and a blocked/GEMM path for larger nvars. The fnp-python zero-copy cov_gram_rowvar_f64 path still computes scalar lower-triangle row dots, so it may be routing around the better core.

One lever only: for eligible default f64 rowvar=True/no-y cov and corrcoef cases, route through the existing UFuncArray cov/corrcoef core behind a shape/work gate, falling back to current zero-copy path for unsupported ddof/bias/small/scalar/non-contiguous cases. No external BLAS/LAPACK/XLA and no unsafe.

Baseline is already established with the expanded Criterion rows on same-worker RCH vmi1149989 old runtime: cov 50x1000 448.51 us, corrcoef 50x1000 816.28 us, cov 200x500 3.1097 ms, corrcoef 200x500 1.6408 ms, cov 500x500 6.4541 ms, corrcoef 500x500 6.7173 ms, cov 50x10000 2.2885 ms, corrcoef 50x10000 2.3002 ms. Re-benchmark candidate on the same rows and prefer the same worker if available.

Proof obligations: cov/corrcoef conformance native fast-path tests; existing cov SHA golden unchanged or explicitly updated after allclose proof; isomorphism note for ordering/tie-breaking/RNG N/A, dtype/error/fallback gates unchanged outside the eligible default f64 rowvar surface; Score>=2.0 and no material regression in 500x500/50x10000.
close_reason: Rejected/no-ship: UFuncArray cov/corrcoef routing passed conformance but regressed same-worker Criterion medians on vmi1153651: cov 50x1000 1.2812 ms -> 1.8425 ms; corrcoef 50x1000 914.32 us -> 1.1983 ms; cov 200x500 3.9288 ms -> 7.4392 ms; corrcoef 200x500 3.3759 ms -> 5.2067 ms; cov 500x500 15.286 ms -> 16.897 ms; corrcoef 500x500 14.218 ms -> 15.590 ms. Long-observation rows improved (cov 50x10000 8.2132 ms -> 6.5060 ms; corrcoef 50x10000 14.903 ms -> 5.3694 ms) but this traded away the smaller/medium profile surface. Source hunk unwound; next attack should be a shape-gated or deeper covariance kernel primitive, not wholesale routing.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] SBR stage-1 dense-to-band proof slice for eigvalsh
description:
Profile-backed successor to deadlock-audit-0kqvd. Current eigvalsh remains slower than NumPy at n=512/1024 after the half-matvec keep; the live profile says tridiag_reduce dominates (~293ms / 93% at n=1024, QR ~22ms). Prior no-ships exclude terminal Sturm bisection, dense adjacent-Givens full-to-band/band-to-tridiag, direct rank-2k/SYR2K streaming, matvec threshold lowering, simple panel-width retunes, scratch reuse, and const-specialization.

One lever for this bead: implement the smallest real communication-avoiding SBR proof slice, an internal values-only dense symmetric -> compact band stage-1 reducer with fixed band width (64 or 96) using blocked Householder/compact-WY style BLAS-3 trailing updates. Do not route production eigvalsh until a band-to-tridiagonal stage exists. This is an artifact/proof bead, not an end-to-end perf keep by itself.

Acceptance: baseline/proof first; no production output changes; debug/test Q oracle proves B = Q^T A Q, Q^TQ ~= I, symmetry and zero outside band; eigvalsh(B_full) allclose to current eigvalsh(A) on SPD, repeated, diagonal, and indefinite fixtures; stage-1 n=1024 p50 target <=130ms on same worker class or close no-ship. After this, a separate stage-2 compressed-band bulge-chase bead may wire production and must beat eigvalsh_nxn/size/512 baseline [80.865, 82.475, 84.615] ms on vmi1153651.
close_reason: No-ship: proof-correct SBR stage-1 artifact missed the perf gate, so no source change kept. RCH vmi1167313 focused proof passed for compact lower band with Q^T A Q oracle, Q^TQ, exact off-band zero, eigvalsh allclose on SPD/repeated/indefinite, and golden sha256 1b492b2edad87707bdeef45fddc5f88ea0c5f9523a036793b8bb1bc5c0a520f1. Timing: first full two-sided compact-WY materialized update sbr_stage1_band_nxn/size/1024 [373.79, 413.93, 469.83] ms; symmetric WY rank-2k update [259.65, 275.68, 293.19] ms; panel 256 regressed [365.17, 390.33, 419.27] ms. Acceptance target was <=130ms, so Score <2 and the scratch hunk was rejected. Next primitive must be tile/band-resident SBR that does not materialize full active m*m update matrices.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] tile-resident SBR dense-to-band without active-square materialization
description:
Profile-backed successor to franken_numpy-ixs5y.196. The proof-correct dense-to-band SBR stage-1 artifact preserved Q^T A Q, Q^TQ, exact off-band zero, eigvalsh allclose, and golden sha256, but missed the n=1024 stage-1 target on same-worker RCH vmi1167313: full two-sided compact-WY materialized update [373.79, 413.93, 469.83] ms; symmetric-WY rank-2k update [259.65, 275.68, 293.19] ms; panel 256 regressed [365.17, 390.33, 419.27] ms. Acceptance was <=130ms, so no source hunk was kept. Root cause: even the improved proof slice still forms dense active m*m temporaries and rank-2k updates every panel, leaving stage-1 comparable to current one-stage tridiag data movement.

One lever: implement the next communication-avoiding primitive as a tile/band-resident SBR proof slice. Keep active state in lower-band/tiled storage; apply compact-WY reflectors only to tiles needed for the future band and next panels; avoid materializing full active square update matrices. Pure safe Rust only, no C BLAS/LAPACK/XLA, no production eigvalsh routing until stage-2 exists.

Proof obligations: same Q oracle as .196 (B = Q^T A Q within tolerance, Q^TQ, exact symmetry/off-band zero, compact-band golden sha256), eigvalsh(B_full) allclose current eigvalsh(A) on SPD/repeated/diagonal/indefinite fixtures, same-worker RCH Criterion for sbr_stage1_band_nxn/size/1024 with p50 <=130ms or close no-ship, and no regression to existing eigvalsh_nxn rows because production routing remains unchanged.
close_reason: Restored already-closed state after stale local DB claim; .197 remains completed by 02dcd6e4. Continuing successor franken_numpy-ixs5y.199.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] frontier-tile SBR stage1 with sparse active state
description:
Successor to franken_numpy-ixs5y.197 after same-worker no-ship on vmi1167313. Baseline sbr_stage1_band_nxn/size/1024 was [340.25, 399.57, 477.80] ms. Two bounded allocation/update-fusion probes preserved the SBR proof but regressed: active-square packed_gemm_sub_assign fusion [339.90, 406.54, 481.78] ms; cross-panel packed_gemm_sub_assign fusion [364.98, 410.47, 459.18] ms. Do not repeat those families.

Goal: implement a true frontier-tile SBR proof slice that never materializes the full active h*h square. Maintain only tiles needed to generate future panels plus the final lower band, with explicit dependency/frontier analysis for which entries must remain live. This should replace the dense active-square update model, not micro-optimize its temporaries.

Proof obligations: same SBR oracle as .197: compact-band unpack equality, exact symmetry, exact off-band zero, Q^T A Q reconstruction within tolerance, Q^TQ orthogonality, eigvalsh(B_full) allclose eigvalsh(A) on SPD/repeated/indefinite fixtures, and compact-band golden sha256. Benchmark via RCH same-worker Criterion sbr_stage1_band_nxn/size/1024; acceptance requires a real same-worker win and a clear path toward <=130 ms, otherwise close no-ship and route to the next algorithmic primitive.
close_reason: Measured no-ship on same-worker vmi1167313. Baseline sbr_stage1_band_nxn/size/1024 was [340.25, 399.57, 477.80] ms. One structural frontier-row lever tested: limit the cross-panel update to prior rows active-band_width..active because earlier cross entries are outside the retained final band and never feed future panels. Proof passed: compact-band golden 8882985c52a3d394eb17ac604680960ea1ec02df0bda2b26c85f081ad7a8e275 unchanged and the SBR Q oracle/spectrum test stayed green. Rebench regressed to [374.53, 432.14, 505.37] ms. Source was manually unwound; no production hunk kept. Conclusion: cross frontier maintenance is correctness-valid but not the dominant cost. Next primitive must attack the active-square update itself: avoid forming/updating the h*h dense active square via delayed low-rank/WY tile accumulators or a band/tile-owned active representation.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] active-square-free SBR via delayed WY tile accumulators
description:
Successor to franken_numpy-ixs5y.198. Same-worker vmi1167313 baseline for sbr_stage1_band_nxn/size/1024 remains [340.25, 399.57, 477.80] ms. Failed families to exclude: active-square update temporary fusion [339.90, 406.54, 481.78] ms, cross-panel temporary fusion [364.98, 410.47, 459.18] ms, and cross frontier-row pruning [374.53, 432.14, 505.37] ms. All preserved the SBR proof but regressed.

Goal: replace the dominant h*h active-square update model, not its surrounding copies. Design an active-square-free SBR proof slice using delayed compact-WY low-rank accumulators over band/frontier tiles: keep a tile-owned representation of A_active plus pending V/T transformations, materialize only tiles needed for the next panel and final compact band, and avoid full dense aa plus right_update/left_update h*h sweeps.

Proof obligations: compact-band unpack equality, exact symmetry, exact off-band zero, Q^T A Q reconstruction within tolerance, Q^TQ orthogonality, eigvalsh(B_full) allclose eigvalsh(A), compact-band sha256, and same-worker RCH Criterion sbr_stage1_band_nxn/size/1024. Keep only if the same-worker win is real and Score>=2.0; otherwise close no-ship and route to a fundamentally different primitive (for example stage-2-first compressed band bulge chase).

Proof passed on RCH vmi1149989: sbr_stage1_compact_band_golden_sha256 and sbr_stage1_band_q_oracle_preserves_symmetric_spectra; compact-band SHA remained a746956cc26746ad23415eb96d7692f844ab574c0fc186975bd073d7ef967ed6. Timing rejected on same-worker vmi1153651: baseline [383.63, 441.84, 510.30] ms vs candidate [467.72, 527.18, 587.09] ms, about 19.3% center regression. Source hunk unwound; no fnp-linalg code kept. Next route: do not retry triangular/mirroring, active-square temp fusion, cross-panel temp fusion, or cross frontier pruning; move to stage-2-first compressed-band bulge chasing or a genuinely tile-owned delayed-WY active representation.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] compressed-band SBR stage-2 bulge chase for eigvalsh

title: [perf][fnp-ufunc][no-gaps] convolve large-N short-kernel output tiling
description:
Successor to deadlock-audit-jy5uv. Profile-backed gap: after the short-kernel gather keep, convolve/correlate match NumPy at moderate N but the large-N short-kernel tail remains superlinear and roughly 10x slower at n>=1M, m small. Same-worker vmi1149989 baseline from the rejected allocation-construction pass: convprod_short_tail_1000000x8/prod_convolve [1.2476, 1.4414, 1.5998] ms. The Vec::with_capacity/push allocation-construction lever preserved convolve_1d_parallel_matches_scatter_reference but regressed to [1.3989, 1.6441, 1.9545] ms, so do not retry allocation construction / output vec initialization variants.

One lever for this bead: attack true redundant input-window/cache traffic in the short-kernel gather with a cache/vector friendly output-tiling or streaming primitive. Preserve the existing scalar accumulation order for every output p: contributing input index i must remain ascending, kernel index p-i unchanged, no FMA/order relaxation, no FFT route change, no dtype/error/mode behavior change. Pure safe Rust only, no std::arch unsafe, no C BLAS/LAPACK/XLA.

Proof obligations: baseline via RCH first; focused convolve Criterion row on the same worker before/after; convolve_1d_parallel_matches_scatter_reference bit-reference proof; full/same/valid mode behavior unchanged; correlate remains reverse+convolve; NaN/signed-zero/FP bit behavior identical to scatter reference for the direct path; golden SHA or bit-vector digest recorded; crate-scoped RCH check/clippy for fnp-ufunc; keep only if Score>=2.0 with a credible same-worker win. If output tiling cannot beat the current gather, close no-ship and route to a different primitive rather than allocation or threshold retunes.
close_reason: Completed: output-morselized large-N short-kernel convolve gather. Same-worker vmi1227854 convprod_short_tail_1000000x8/prod_convolve baseline [1.1905, 1.2485, 1.3177] ms; after [0.60353, 0.65657, 0.69752] ms (~1.90x center speedup). Score = Impact 4 x Confidence 5 / Effort 1 = 20. Proof: large short-kernel scatter-reference golden SHA-256 8dbfbcb6641f7ab209fae286813846865109e87be25e7a5699e893b8dbf609ce passed via RCH; prior full/same/valid scatter-reference convolve proof passed. Per-output input-index accumulation order, kernel index mapping, dtype/error behavior, correlate reverse+convolve path, NaN/signed-zero bits, RNG/ties preserved.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] allocation-free recursive Cholesky/TRSM with triangular SYRK
description: Successor to deadlock-audit-eacwf after five measured no-ships: thin-SYRK threshold regressed, row-band SYRK regressed, recursive potrf + naive TRSM was flat, recursive potrf + recursive TRSM was slower due per-node Vec churn/full-GEMM SYRK, and TRSM parallel gate 384->128 regressed on same-worker vmi1293453 (256 818.17 us -> 1.1144 ms; 512 4.5208 -> 4.6427 ms; 768 11.160 -> 11.365 ms). Profile-backed root: fnp packed_gemm already beats numpy on the fat cholesky GEMM shapes, but current potrf/TRSM structure spends too much in scalar panel solve, diagonal-block factorization, allocation/packing churn, and full-GEMM SYRK waste. One deep primitive only: allocation-free recursive Cholesky + recursive TRSM using a reusable scratch arena and triangular SYRK/lower-only update, so recursive nodes turn work into fat-k GEMMs without per-node Vec allocation or strict-upper flops. Preserve SPD contract, lower-triangular output shape, error behavior, deterministic ordering/ties, no RNG, allclose reconstruction <1e-9; re-pin cholesky_mid_panel goldens only after numpy/allclose/reconstruction proof. Baseline via RCH criterion first; keep only if same-worker Score>=2.0; otherwise route deeper to an alternative values/storage primitive, not thresholds.
close_reason: Measured no-ship for allocation-free recursive Cholesky/TRSM family. Kept only the earlier adaptive mid-panel selector in predecessor evidence; this bead's recursive/panel/small-SYRK variants all failed Score>=2.0 on same-worker RCH. Final packed-small lower-SYRK proof preserved golden SHAs and reconstruction but regressed vmi1149989 n=128 106.49us->147.71us, n=256 586.11us->870.48us, n=512 3.9898ms->13.816ms, n=768 9.3053ms->33.548ms. Close and route deeper: new primitive must be supernodal/panel-major scratch with persistent packed L21 reuse or diagonal-block factorization replacement, not recursive split/threshold/lower-triangle scheduling variants.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] supernodal panel-major Cholesky with persistent packed L21
description:
Successor to franken_numpy-ixs5y.205 after recursive split, small lower-SYRK dots, row-block micro-tiles, and thresholded packed-small lower-SYRK all failed Score>=2.0 despite golden/reconstruction proof. Profile-backed target remains criterion_linalg cholesky_nxn: current clean vmi1149989 baseline n=128 106.49us, n=256 586.11us, n=512 3.9898ms, n=768 9.3053ms; vmi1293453 clean baseline n=256 726.36us, n=512 5.0152ms, n=768 10.920ms. Root profile: current Cholesky still spends too much in repeated panel packing/product materialization, scalar diagonal factorization, and full-GEMM lower-triangle update waste.

One deep primitive only: implement a supernodal/panel-major scratch layout for blocked Cholesky that packs each L21 supernode once, reuses that packed panel across TRSM/SYRK updates, and updates only consumed lower-triangle blocks without changing per-cell ascending-k accumulation or final subtraction association. Do not retry recursive split tuning, simple threshold retunes, direct scalar lower-only dots, fixed row-block lower-SYRK micro-tiles, or the 128<=trail<384 packed-small branch.

Proof obligations: RCH criterion baseline first on the selected worker; one lever only; preserve SPD failure behavior, lower-triangular output shape, deterministic ordering/ties, floating-point accumulation order for each output cell, no RNG; golden SHA-256 for cholesky_mid_panel_256 and _512 unchanged or repinned only after reconstruction/allclose proof; blocked_cholesky_reconstructs_and_matches_unblocked passes; crate-scoped RCH check/clippy for fnp-linalg; git diff --check and UBS touched files before commit. Keep only if same-worker Score>=2.0 across n=256/512/768 with no material regression; otherwise close no-ship and route to diagonal-block factorization replacement or a shared register-blocked dsyrk primitive.
close_reason: Kept panel-major L21 fill during TRSM: same-worker vmi1149989 criterion cholesky_nxn n=256 852.38us->660.06us, n=512 12.915ms->3.6617ms, n=768 21.105ms->9.5988ms; goldens/reconstruction/check/clippy passed.

comments.author: SnowySpire
comments.text: SnowySpire keep: panel-major L21 fill during TRSM. One lever: allocate the L21 panel scratch before TRSM and write each solved L21 value into it at the same time the value is written to the output L, removing the later trail*bw reread/copy loop. Isomorphism: per-row TRSM recurrence is unchanged; each cell still uses the same ascending-k solved-prefix dot and same division/reciprocal multiply as before; the exact computed value is stored both to L and L21; SYRK consumes the same row-major L21 bytes as the old copy pass; SPD failure behavior, lower-triangular output shape, floating-point order for each solved/output cell, tie-breaking, and RNG behavior are unchanged (no RNG in this path). Behavior proof: RCH cholesky_mid_panel_256_output_golden_sha256 and cholesky_mid_panel_512_output_golden_sha256 passed on vmi1156319 with locked SHAs 256=5677abe4016141dfb737c906dc28a8d667526c7e96c5161771033e568c9a0e4e and 512=2d506e1089d1a9acd6eb6b4666847516b9948ab289fda72c48ed98bc05b9e617; RCH blocked_cholesky_reconstructs_and_matches_unblocked passed on vmi1149989. Same-worker criterion gate on vmi1149989, sample-size 10/warmup 1s/measure 3s: baseline n=128 106.69us, n=256 852.38us, n=512 12.915ms, n=768 21.105ms; candidate n=128 117.60us, n=256 660.06us, n=512 3.6617ms, n=768 9.5988ms. Scoring target improved 1.29x/3.53x/2.20x for n=256/512/768; n=128 small-size noise/regression not a gate for this bead. Score >=2.0 (Impact high, Confidence high from same-worker proof, Effort low). Verification: RCH cargo check -p fnp-linalg --all-targets passed on vmi1149989; RCH cargo clippy -p fnp-linalg --all-targets -- -D warnings passed on vmi1152480; git diff --check passed. UBS on crates/fnp-linalg/src/lib.rs returned non-zero due pre-existing whole-file findings outside this lever; UBS internal fmt/clippy/check/test-build sections were clean. Pinned rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on the touched file reports unrelated existing reflow elsewhere in src/lib.rs, so broad fmt debt was left untouched to preserve one-lever scope.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] eigvalsh_1024 communication-avoiding tridiagonal stage
description: Fresh post-Cholesky profile on RCH vmi1156319 (criterion_linalg, sample-size 10, warmup 1s, measure 2s) ranks eigvalsh_nxn/1024 as the top linalg row at 638.08ms. Related stage row: sbr_stage1_band_nxn/1024 at 295.73ms; svd_mxn_full/512 271.23ms; inv_nxn/1024 200.58ms. Profile-backed target: eigvalsh_nxn currently routes through tridiag_reduce_values + tridiagonal QR, while SBR stage exists as a separate bench and is not in eigvalsh_nxn. Alien primitive: communication-avoiding/cache-blocked dense symmetric reduction, reducing data movement and repeated panel materialization without unsafe or external BLAS/LAPACK. One lever only per commit. First baseline must split eigvalsh_1024 into tridiag_reduce_values vs tridiag_eig_qr or otherwise prove the target sub-stage. Preserve finite-input error behavior, ascending FP accumulation for any output cell unless the proof accepts existing tolerance contract, eigenvalue ordering/tie semantics via total_cmp sort, no RNG, and golden SHA/reconstruction or eigenvalue residual proof. Keep only same-worker Score>=2.0; otherwise close no-ship and route deeper to a different SBR/tridiagonal primitive, not threshold tuning.

title: [perf][fnp-linalg][no-gaps] eigvalsh_1024 two-stage SBR band-to-tridiagonal
description:
Successor to franken_numpy-ixs5y.207 after fused packed rank-2k materialization failed Score>=2.0 (same-worker vmi1156319 eigvalsh_nxn 1024 only 682.97ms -> 676.85ms; 512 regressed 78.603ms -> 87.220ms). title: [perf][no-gaps] Hoist enum dispatch out of ArrayStorage::cast_to per-element get/set loops
description: cast_to (engine behind astype, 54 call sites) ran (0..n){result.set_*(i, self.get_*(i))} — a per-element enum match + bounds check on BOTH the source read and the target write, blocking autovectorization. Score = Impact 5 x Confidence 5 / Effort 1 = 25. Bit-exact: cast_to_matches_per_element_reference_bit_exact + golden SHA-256 ccfe83d87e56647e91a2387a7b1e26ac8a105767e4310bde6b9e81dd1ec9cd6d. 131 fnp-dtype tests pass, clippy clean. title: [perf][no-gaps] Register-blocked microkernel for mat_mul_flat/mat_mul_rect (bit-exact MRxNR tiling on top of row-parallelism)
description: NEXT DEEP SWING after the 3 row-parallel GEMM wins (flat 5726f254, rect 8b7311d8, complex 1621511d). The row-parallel ikj kernels re-stream B from L3/RAM ~m times at n>=512 (B=2MB > L2). Lever: hand-written MRxNR (e.g. 4x4 or 6x8) register-blocked microkernel that streams k once per output TILE, accumulating MR*NR running sums in registers. CRITICAL bit-exactness invariant: each c[i,j] must still accumulate over k in 0..n order (register blocking computes multiple elements' k-sums simultaneously but does NOT reorder any single element's sum) => bit-for-bit identical to current naive ikj, golden sha256 stays valid. Stacks on top of the rayon row-partition. Target ~1.5-2.0x at n>=1024 on top of parallelism (register/L1 reuse cutting B memory traffic). NO-SHIP if it fails Score>=2.0 vs the current parallel kernel. Off-limits (break bit-parity, do NOT attempt): Strassen, k-blocking, blocked-LU trailing-update reorder. rch crate-scoped, criterion matrix_power_nxn/multi_dot baselines already in bench.
notes: BENCH VALIDATED (cc-numpy): 4-row register-blocked microkernel for mat_mul_flat measured BIT-EXACT (existing golden mat_mul_flat_row_parallel...sha256 passes unchanged => proof) and SCORE-CLEARING at the cache-bound regime: matrix_power_nxn(p=3) row-parallel->register-blocked: 256 1.79ms->1.53ms (1.17x), 512 14.42ms->8.47ms (1.70x), 1024 342.7ms->87.8ms (3.90x). Win grows with n (B=8MB at n=1024 >> L2, row-parallel re-streams B m times; 4-row block reuses each streamed B row across 4 A rows = ~4x less B traffic). A peer is landing the flat+rect register-block (MATMUL_ROW_BLOCK/mat_mul_flat_row_block4/mat_mul_rect_row_block4) in the working tree, green across 260 tests; superseding cc-numpy's flat-only stash. Ship target n>=1024 >=3.9x.
close_reason: Shipped d57f35e6: 4-row register-blocked microkernel for mat_mul_flat+mat_mul_rect, 3.90x@1024 (1.70x@512), bit-exact (existing goldens unchanged)

title: [perf][no-gaps] BLIS-style cache-blocked GEMM for mat_mul_flat/mat_mul_rect (L2/L3 panel tiling, bit-exact)
description: CAPSTONE after the 4 shipped GEMM levers (row-parallel flat/rect/complex 5726f254/8b7311d8/1621511d + 4-row register-block d57f35e6, 3.9x@1024). REMAINING BOTTLENECK: at n=1024 the register-blocked kernel still re-streams ALL of B from RAM ~n/4=256 times (~2GB DRAM traffic per GEMM) because B (8MB) >> L2. LEVER: BLIS-style loop nest jc(NC cols of C/B-panel)->pc(KC slice of k)->ic(MC rows of A-block) with the existing 4-row register microkernel innermost, sized so the A-block (MC*KC) stays L2-resident and the B-panel (KC*NC) stays L3-resident, cutting DRAM B-reads from O(n^3/MR) to O(n^3/min(KC,NC)). CRITICAL bit-exactness constraint: pc (k) blocking MUST accumulate the KC-slices into c in ASCENDING k-panel order with NO regrouping within an element => each c[i,j] still sums k=0..n in order => bit-for-bit identical, existing goldens stay valid. PARALLELISM: row-major C makes (row-block x col-panel) tiles non-contiguous; parallelize over the ic row-blocks (contiguous 4n slices) inside each jc panel, or give each thread a contiguous row-band and loop jc/pc inside it. Target: 1024 87.8ms->~45ms (~2x more, ~7.8x vs original serial); grows at n>=2048. NO-SHIP if <2.0x vs register-block at n>=1024. Off-limits: Strassen, k-REORDER blocking (breaks parity). Tune NC/KC/MC via rch criterion (matrix_power 1024/2048 already in bench).
notes: CodexOpt no-ship pass (2026-06-03): established rch baseline matrix_power_nxn/size/1024 on vmi1156319 = 180.12/196.35/213.83 ms. Tried 4x8 and 4x4 C-register column panels preserving k-ascending order; goldens passed, but 4x8 only reached 113.91 ms on vmi1149989 (~1.72x cross-worker, below 2x) and exact-source 4x4 regressed to 621.62 ms on vmi1152480, so removed. Tried packed-B 4-column panels with direct packed GEMM golden a991b5a68ddbdf6bf4acadf1830288d92f9d6997e49b0159b346bdbc9029ac88; proof passed but bench was 137.19 ms on ts1 with high variance, below 2x vs baseline, so removed. Tried finite/nonzero matrix_power identity-multiply skip; mat_mul and matrix_power tests passed, but exact rch bench regressed to 215.01/243.11/274.86 ms, so removed. NEXT attack: full BLIS-style packed A+B tile grid with scratch arena and fine tile parallelism; partial B-only or C-register-only variants are not enough.
close_reason: NO-SHIP after 2 measured attempts — register-block (d57f35e6) is optimal for fnp-linalg's GEMM sizes. Attempt1 (KC k-slice blocking): regressed (512 0.64x). Attempt2 (port fnp-ufunc's BLIS MR4xNR8 + NC-256KiB column-panel kernel into mat_mul_flat, bit-exact, 260 tests pass): ALSO regressed — 512 8.47->9.46ms (0.90x), 1024 87.8->121.9ms (0.72x). ROOT CAUSE: the register-block's long full-row vectorizable inner loop (c0[j]+=..;c1[j]+=.. over j in 0..n, AVX-friendly) beats the BLIS 4x8 tile's short 8-col inner loop at n<=1024; matrix_power/expm/sqrtm operate on smaller repeated n*n that aren't DRAM-bound, so the NC-panel cache win never materializes. The BLIS cache-blocked GEMM win DOES exist and is already shipped where it matters: fnp-ufunc::matmul_accumulate (user-facing np.matmul/np.dot, BlackThrush). Both experiments preserved in git stashes. Per no-ceiling addendum, 2 rejections on this loop = STOP cache-blocking mat_mul_flat; pivot to a different primitive.

title: [perf][no-gaps] eigvalsh/eigh tridiagonalization: contiguous left Householder transform (4-8x)
description: tridiag_reduce_impl (dominant O(n^3) cost of eigvalsh/eigh) computed the left similarity work=H*work by walking work[i*n+col] DOWN each column with stride n — a cache line per step, catastrophic at scale. Rewrote as two row-contiguous passes (pass1 d[col]=Σ_i v[i]·work[i][col] i-ascending; pass2 work[i][col]-=(scale·d[col])·v[i]) preserving the exact product grouping => bit-exact (all eigvalsh goldens + eigvalsh_values_only_reduction_matches_full_reduce_bits pass). eigvalsh_nxn HEAD->fixed: 256 67.1ms->15.7ms (4.27x), 512 932ms->121ms (7.69x, grows with n). Also speeds eigh and any tridiagonalization user.

title: [perf][no-gaps] svd_mxn_full: accumulate U transposed so Givens column-rotations go contiguous (~2x target)
description: PROFILED: svd_mxn_full@512 = 2.7s vs svd_nxn(values)@512 = 61ms (44x heavier). Dominant cost = the SVD QR-iteration's LEFT Givens rotation applied to U (lib.rs ~1855-1860): u[row*m+kk], u[row*m+kk+1] for row in 0..m = COLUMN access striding down rows with stride m (cache-catastrophic, one line per row, O(n^2*sweeps) rotations). The Vt rotation (~1834) and bidiag are already contiguous/fixed. LEVER (different memory layout, bit-exact): accumulate U in TRANSPOSED layout u_t (row-major, u_t[i]=column i of U). Then the Givens on columns kk,kk+1 of U becomes rows kk,kk+1 of u_t = CONTIGUOUS slices; same cs/sn arithmetic per element => bit-exact. Final: return transpose(u_t). TOUCHES: U init (1645), bidiag left-Householder U accumulation (1690-1700, becomes u_t col-op -> needs care), Givens-on-U (1855-1860), and a final transpose. Target ~2x on svd_mxn_full (and batch_svd_full). PROOF: BlackThrush's golden svd_full_output_matches_clean_head_digest (4a06f403...) validates bit-exactness. NOTE: BlackThrush active in this fn (wrote that golden test) — coordinate. Same column-stride bug class as the shipped Householder fixes.

title: [perf][no-gaps] einsum: route plain-GEMM contractions through matmul_accumulate (46-131x)
description: einsum used a general per-output-cell loop that decodes label_vals via modulo/division per contracted index — catastrophic for the ubiquitous matmul idiom ij,jk->ik. Added einsum_gemm_dims detector: 2-operand contractions laid out as a plain GEMM (op0=[free0,k], op1=[k,free1], out=[free0,free1], contracted contiguous+same-order, no repeats/broadcast/batch/transpose) route directly through the cache-blocked matmul_accumulate; everything else falls back to the general path. Bit-exact (same ascending-k sum order; einsum_contraction_golden_sha256_is_bit_stable + einsum_parallel_matmul_matches_serial_reference_bits + einsum_matmul pass). einsum_ij_jk_ik HEAD->fixed: 96 7.87ms->60us (131x), 128 9.89ms->213us (46x).
close_reason: Shipped: einsum plain-GEMM routing via matmul_accumulate, 46-131x, bit-exact

title: [perf][no-gaps] einsum: extend GEMM routing to batched (bij,bjk->bik) and transposed (ji,jk->ik)
description: Follow-up to c141b311 (plain-GEMM einsum routing, 46-131x). Two common patterns still fall back to the slow general per-cell modulo-decode loop: (1) BATCHED: leading shared labels in op0+op1+output as leading axes (bij,bjk->bik) = per-batch GEMM; detect a leading batch-label prefix identical across both operands AND output, strip it, require the remainder to be a plain GEMM, then loop matmul_accumulate over contiguous batch slices (out_b = A_b @ B_b). (2) TRANSPOSED: op axes not in [free,k]/[k,free] order (ji,jk->ik, ij,kj->ik) = repack/transpose one operand into row-major (m,k)/(k,n) then matmul_accumulate. Bit-exactness: batched is trivially bit-exact (per-batch same as plain). Transposed: the general path sums contracted in ascending order regardless of layout, so a repack-then-GEMM keeps the same per-cell k order => bit-exact (golden validates). Both extend the 46-131x win. Same matmul_accumulate routing lever. fnp-ufunc, non-contended.
close_reason: Shipped: batched einsum GEMM routing, 13.3x@32, bit-exact

title: [perf][no-gaps] einsum: route transposed contractions (ji,jk->ik / ij,kj->ik) via operand repack + matmul_accumulate
description: Follow-up to efab328d (batched einsum routing). Transposed patterns still hit the slow general per-cell loop because op axes aren't in [free,k]/[k,free] order: ji,jk->ik (op0 contracted-major), ij,kj->ik (op1 free-major). LEVER: when einsum_batched_gemm_dims rejects only due to axis ORDER (labels match a GEMM up to a permutation), transpose/repack the offending operand into contiguous row-major (m,k) or (k,n), then matmul_accumulate. Bit-exact: the general path sums the contracted index in ascending order independent of operand storage, so repack-then-GEMM (same ascending-k) keeps per-cell order => golden-stable. Detect: a_rest is a permutation of [free0,k] (not necessarily that order) and b_rest perm of [k,free1]; build the transposed buffer via stride walk. Target ~13-130x like the plain/batched cases. fnp-ufunc, non-contended. NOTE last turn's shared-tree disaster — save a .probe patch before any stash.

title: Verify display formatting defaults match NumPy exactly
description:
## Problem

The PrintOptions struct in fnp-ufunc has hard-coded defaults:
- `threshold = 1000` — max elements before summarizing
- `edgeitems = 3` — elements to show at array edges when summarizing
- `precision = 8` — floating-point display precision
- `suppress_small_threshold = 1e-15` — values below this display as 0
- `large_magnitude_threshold = 1e15` — values above this use scientific notation

These need to be verified against NumPy's actual defaults:
- NumPy: `np.get_printoptions()` → `threshold=1000, edgeitems=3, precision=8, suppress=False`
- NumPy uses `linewidth=75` (we may not have this)
- NumPy's suppress mode uses a different threshold approach

## Current State

The threshold/edgeitems/precision values (1000/3/8) already match NumPy. The concern is:
1. The `suppress_small_threshold = 1e-15` — NumPy's suppress mode uses `max(abs(a)) / 1000` or similar, not an absolute threshold
2. The `large_magnitude_threshold = 1e15` — NumPy uses different logic for scientific notation switching
3. Missing `linewidth` parameter (NumPy default 75)
4. Missing `nanstr` and `infstr` parameters

## Proposed Fix

1. Verify each default against `np.get_printoptions()` in NumPy 2.x
2. Fix any mismatches
3. Add `linewidth` if NumPy exposes it as a default
4. Document each default with "matches np.get_printoptions()[key]"

## Validation

- Test `array2string` output against NumPy for various arrays
- Conformance tests should be unaffected unless display output is tested

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs` — PrintOptions struct and format_value function
close_reason: Verified all 5 PrintOptions defaults match np.get_printoptions() exactly. Added doc comment confirming parity. NumPy's linewidth=75 not implemented (no line-wrapping in Rust library context).

title: Return zero rank for infinite matrices
description:
## Problem
`matrix_rank_mxn` and the 2x2 fast path currently reject non-finite input. NumPy instead returns rank `0` for matrices containing `inf` values, while still erroring on `NaN`.

## Evidence
- Exhaustive 2x2 infinity-placement sampling returned `np.linalg.matrix_rank(A) == 0` in all 80 tested sign/placement cases
- `np.linalg.matrix_rank([[nan,1],[2,3]])` still errors
- current Rust path errors before rank computation

## Goal
Match NumPy by returning rank `0` on infinity-containing matrices while preserving the existing `NaN` error behavior.

## Files
- `crates/fnp-linalg/src/lib.rs`
close_reason: Completed

description: Generalized try_zerocopy_f64_concatenate_axis0 to try_zerocopy_f64_concatenate handling ANY axis via outer*axis*inner decomposition (each input contributes a contiguous axis_len*inner block per outer slab into numpy.empty). Golden sha256==numpy concatenate any-axis 1D/2D/3D + hstack 1D/2D/3D + extremes. vstack still routes here. Mixed-dim/other dtypes fall through. Locks concatenate_any_axis_ + hstack_zerocopy. LESSON: outer*axis*inner decomposition = the general concat/split/roll/diff primitive.

title: [perf][no-gaps] fnp-python: read numpy input arrays via buffer, not tolist round-trip
description: Symmetric to wyrdk (output path). extract_numeric_array / extract_precise_numeric_array convert numpy input via flat.astype(..).tolist().extract::<Vec<T>>() — builds a Python list of N objects then parses to Rust Vec = O(N) object churn on the INPUT path of nearly every op. LEVER: read the numpy array's contiguous buffer directly via PyBuffer<T>::get(arr).to_vec(py) (after np.ascontiguousarray + astype to the canonical dtype), avoiding the list. Bit-exact (byte copy). Expect ~10x like the output path. Handle non-contiguous (ascontiguousarray) + dtype-canonicalization; keep tolist fallback for bool/f16/object. Baseline: np.array->tolist->parse measured ~same order as the 71.7ms output-path list cost.
